seoTitle: Longwood vs Louth Bay — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Longwood and Louth Bay. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Longwood vs Louth Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Longwood and Louth Bay.
heading: Longwood vs Louth Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Longwood, South Australia and Louth Bay, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Louth Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Longwood (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Longwood or Louth Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Longwood has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Louth Bay scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Longwood scores 63/100 for bushfire risk. Louth Bay scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Longwood or Louth Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Louth Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
